title: Rejoin-traffic and undercut-feasibility proxies from position congestion
summary: Public F1 strategy references emphasize traffic, warm-up, and undercut/overcut economics, but your strongest features mostly encode tire-life service windows.
rationale: Public F1 strategy references emphasize traffic, warm-up, and undercut/overcut economics, but your strongest features mostly encode tire-life service windows. A driver may delay a stop not because tires are fine, but because pitting now would rejoin into traffic or destroy an overcut.
implementation_hint: Derive proxies from currently available columns only: local position congestion (density of cars by position bucket within race-lap), whether the driver is in a train (small recent position volatility plus poor lap-delta rank), clean-air proxy for front-runners, and undercut-feasibility score combining bad current lap_delta with high relative tyre age and favorable position band. Also test compound-specific warm-up interactions so overcut-friendly compounds/races are treated differently.
expected_effect: Can add orthogonal signal around borderline calls where tire wear alone is ambiguous.
risk: These are indirect proxies because explicit time gaps are absent, so too many handcrafted traffic scores may overfit specific races.
